method_atom: A nonperturbative, threshold-driven instability of a quantum ground state: once an external control field exceeds a critical value, the ground state spontaneously decays by creating correlated pairs of excitations, with a production rate that is exponentially (tunneling-like) suppressed below the threshold. The same critical-threshold pair-production mechanism is then reformulated inside a dynamical, strongly curved background geometry, so that the threshold condition and decay rate are set by the evolving background.
object_atom: Electron-positron pair creation from the QED vacuum in strong electromagnetic fields — the critical-field vacuum-polarization processes of Dirac, Breit-Wheeler and Sauter-Heisenberg-Euler-Schwinger — probed both in Earth-based heavy-ion collisions and in the general-relativistic gravitational collapse of matter to a black hole, as observed in Gamma Ray Bursts.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the critical-field-triggered spontaneous decay of a ground state into pairs and its embedding in a curved dynamical background; the electron-positron QED vacuum and black-hole-collapse setting is the physics-specific object.
